methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $350M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Fairmat, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised approximately €95M ($103M) to date, is valued at $350M post-money. The peer universe comprises five private robotics firms at the same stage—1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and GrayMatter Robotics—alongside one public comparable.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $665M from the current $350M base. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in the robotics sector at this stage; 1X Technologies commands a $10.0B valuation while GrayMatter Robotics stands at $300M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $565M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.2B. This range reflects meaningful tension between stage-based progression and peer-relative positioning. The current $350M valuation sits below the stage-progression midpoint and substantially below peer median, suggesting either a discount relative to comparables or slower capital deployment relative to peers. Key uncertainties include capital intensity relative to peer cohorts and the applicability of outliers like 1X Technologies to Fairmat's specific subsector focus.
